extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL Warren won Stage 13 on Bastille Day by sitting on QUINTANA/CONTADOR/LANDA in the final breakaway while they chased, then outsprinting them.
tactical_patterns: FROOME attacked on steepest sections of final climb to test form after losing yellow, showing intentional rope-a-dope weakness earlier.
Team SKY sent LANDA in break to forceARU's isolated Astana to chase, inverting traditional Sky defend-with-jersey tactics.
ARU had zero teammates on final climb but survived by exclusively following FROOME wheels, ignoring other attacks completely.
BARGUIL won by tactically sitting on QUINTANA/CONTADOR doing chase work in closing kilometers despite having fresher legs.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: attacked steepest climb sections and descended aggressively after appearing weak all day, reasserting GC contention despite losing yellow
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: kept yellow through wheel-following discipline but Astana team collapse means unsustainable isolation for remainder
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Sky's willingness to send him in break signals confidence in using him offensively rather than defensively for Froome
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: second place after being written off previous day, showing GC recovery despite doing futile chase work for Barguil
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: third place after being on death's door earlier in Tour, aggressive riding signals form returning
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: massive pace work on final climb gained nothing, wasted energy in domestique role while losing GC time
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: tactical maturity to sit on while others chased, won Bastille Day in polka dots with perfect positioning
